reviews.review_text: Great location in the heart of Surfers with beach views, a large balcony overlooking Cavill Avenue and close to trams. Great price, friendly staff and a really nice find.

One of the best hotels in Australia.
Excellent Security Staff on weekends and regularly patrolled throughout the week by Gold Coast Protection.
Highly recommended, 10/10.
酒店位子绝佳！就在海滩边上！楼层高一点房间拍夜景也不错！房间干净 空间也比较大！
위치는 좋고 건물은 그럴듯 했는데, 호텔에 부대시설도 없고 , 수영장도 없음. 너무 낡고.
The condition of the rooms were appalling! Long due for renovation!

From the friendly reception check in, with a lovely bottle of wine, to the room staff who came to help us, when the tv had no signal, to the delicious breakfast this morning as well as, easy express check out. Absolutely perfect!
Run down last updated in the 90s by the looks of it

Hello I’m very dessu I booked for the jacuzzi but from what I understood it and closed I don’t know when the jacuzzi will reopen but even the hotel doesn’t know I would like that if he answers my comment he explains this to me thank you

Friendly staff, lovely food. Definitely recommend. Shouts out to the chef Kane and the 2 hosts Carrie & Lani. Couldnt do enough for us.

Location pretty decent, close enough to all amenities. Room good size, bit dated but clean! Couldn't be helped but lots of construction work happening in front of the hotel so very noisy from 6am.

Great location have stayed many times, unfortunately had a bit of a negative experience. Did not get room service, followed this up with staff and they said someone would come and they never did. Asked for more glasses to come to the room and they never did. Good place but lack of hospitality.
Very good

Booked 4 guest room, staying for one night today; when I checked the bed sheet just before I go to bed, found period stains on bed sheet. Unsure when they last changed those sheets indicating poor service. Very unhappy on that, as we expect a clean bed-space to spend the night for good sleep. Tried calling reception, they did not respond. Please, please do check and make sure you do check on everything at the very moment you enter the room in this hotel. Never had this bad experience before. I wonder if they do clean the rooms properly.

The view from the room is amazing from the floor which i was in, the 16th floor.

Laurice (guest service agent) was great.
She booked us with advise and got my rewards sorted.

I recently had a wonderful stay at Mantra on View Hotel. The experience was excellent from start to finish. The staff were incredibly friendly, welcoming, and always ready to help. A special mention to Chef kane , who was very accommodating and ensured we had a great dining experience.

The food was delicious and well-prepared, and the overall service from both housekeeping and front desk staff was outstanding. Their professionalism and warmth truly made the stay memorable.

If I return to the Gold Coast, I will definitely choose to stay here again.

— Amit Yadav

We booked three separate rooms for our families with kids, excited to use the pool as advertised on the hotel's website. Unfortunately, it was a complete lie - there's no swimming pool on the premises. Instead, they have four different spa pools, none of which were functional during our stay. I spoke to the receptionist about the issue, but he denied that they were spa pools, which was absurd. To make matters worse, there was no lifeguard on duty for safety. We also paid for a breakfast buffet for eight people for five days, but the options were limited and disappointing. Overall, our stay was a letdown, and I wouldn't recommend this hotel to anyone.

We only stayed for one night, but the location and views were fantastic. The room and bed were comfortable; however, the property does feel quite outdated and would benefit from a refresh. In particular, the silicone work around the shower screen was poorly done and needs attention.

It was a lovely touch to be welcomed with a bottle of wine. Breakfast was good—prepared a little differently from the norm, but still enjoyable.

Parking is undercover, though not the most convenient when travelling alone. You need to drop your belongings at the front entrance, take them to your room, then return to park the car and walk back up via the internal stairs.

There is a small kids’ playroom with pay-to-use games. It’s a nice idea, but the cost can add up quickly. I didn’t notice a pool or other facilities, so I’m unsure if there are additional amenities available.

The property was quite busy, likely due to holidays and local events. Despite this, the staff were polite and friendly. I did feel for the front desk staff member who was initially on her own—there was about a 30-minute wait before additional staff came out to assist.

Grandpa and his partner stayed here for 10 days and loved it.

The view from the balcony was amazing, heaps of amenities (three pools!), and great location for all the touristy stuff.

The place was as advertised, a prime location honestly, very much the heart of Surfer’s Paradise. The breakfast buffet was a standout feature, well worth the money.

The only downside I would say would be the cleanliness of the rooms. It wasn’t overwhelming notable, but there were faint stains on the carpets and pillows themselves. Other than that, the furniture could use a fresh coat of paint and the shower fixtures can’t handle the water pressure without damn near flying off.

For the price and location this place is still a steal of a deal and worth your time.
It was a good location close to food, beach and tram stop. It was also affordable for us 3 friends and enough space for our luggage and nice views of the beach

This hotel is not cheap to stay, and it was well below expectations. Firstly, as the photos show, the room was tired, dirty and not at all well presented. I was advised 3 days venture we were arriving that the beach club next door to the hotel is noisy, but with the balcony doors shut the noise is greatly reduced. This was a lie. The beach club (see photos) is right outside the rooms on the southern side of the building. The noise is inescapable from midday till after midnight. The bass, the music volume and the screams and cheers of patrons are all very audible through the closed doors and curtains. Had I known earlier I would never have paid to be kept awake. The room was tired, with dirty curtains, mould, patched/unpainted areas and a very strange glass panel in the bathroom that allows anyone in the lounge area to see whoever is showering!! The location is fabulous, so close to the beach, restaurants, cafes and the light rail, but for close to $800 for two nights with parking (in a public car park next to the hotel and not within the building) I am sure there are better places to stay. Our room was changed en route to the premises with a call made to me, and I assumed this would be a room further away from the beach club and the incessant noise. This was not the case. And although it was a King suite my adult son had to sleep in a roll away bed. Again I would never have accepted the change had I known it was for their room planning rather than to make it more peaceful for us. I will not stay here again, nor do I recommend it to anyone else unless the price is markedly reduced and you don’t like to rest from midday till after midnight in your own room.
No pool - not very family friendly with little kids

I was very disappointed with this hotel. I booked weeks before our stay, and 3 days before our stay I received an email warning me about the volume of noise from a neighbouring bar/pool party facility. I called and asked about the noise and was advised that with the balcony doors shut the noise was minimal. Rubbish. We had our room changed as we drove to the GC from Brisbane and I assumed they were moving us away from the noisy club. We were completely overlooking it. The noise of the music, the bass and the screaming voices of people in the Cali Beach Club was inescapable. The verandah sliding glass doors did little to reduce noise until 12.30am. The room we were moved to was old, tired and showed a lot of wear and tear. For the price of nearly $800 for two nights I thought it would be lovely. The carpet in the room was old and tatty. Cupboard doors were broken, patched up damaged not repainted and the sliding door to the balcony took Herculean efforts to open. The towels we were given had stains and marks, and the shampoo and conditioner dispenser in the shower did not work. Parking is $20 a night but is in a public car park next door to the hotel without direct access. There was no security in the car park such as cameras or staffing. I took photos of the room condition after raising some concerns with staff which were pretty much brushed off. It is a shame because the location is fabulous, the staff when we checked in were pleasant but then it all went downhill from there. I do not recommend this hotel if you need sleep and peace in your room from midday to after midnight when the club operates.
It was awesome stay only a bit unhappy cuz with kids and no pool bit  disappointed .

The hotel is conveniently located at Surfers Paradise. The hotel lacks a swimming pool but proximity to the beach helped. 

The breakfast felt slightly overpriced but lots of options around. 

We will be back again soon.
I found The place to be clean staff, very helpful and in the great location.

Lovely.

Buffet breakfast at Nosh Restaurant Level 2, is definitely worth it! Friendly staff, a good selection of food.

Staff we dealt with were all lovely and understanding of the request to move rooms after I showed them photos. However the room we originally got was absolutely disgusting with stains all over the carpet, and on my pillow case. The furniture was falling apart and missing doors and did not remotely match the photographs on the website listing. We slept so poorly due to a fire alarm first night and on the second night other guests running up and down the hallways screaming (which I recognise is not the hotels fault) which inconjunction with my friend also having a skin reaction to the bedding we had to leave early. Which obviously isn’t ideal given we are out of pocket for that third night.
Good hotel with great location close to the beach. Excellent service!

Service is always 5 star. I can choose from any Accor hotel for my monthly work visits to the Goldy but I choose to stay at Mantra on View. A little bit dated but service is more important. Chef Kane is always quick and efficient and performs his duty with quality cooking and a smile. All staff are friendly, polite and helpful. Would recommend this hotel to anyone coming to Surfers.
The service provided was top notch and the food were amazing. Great location,close to public transport.
Lovely stay. Perfect location, buffet brekky is a must, amazing food 😋
Quick overnight for work visiting clients nearby, rooms are fine, service from reception team friendly

Our experience was excellent. The room was comfortable and well appointed, and the reception staff were courteous and attentive. The food was a highlight of our stay, and Chef Kane provided outstanding service

Great stay, rooms have been refurbished since we last stayed here a few years ago, the rooms are clean and fresh with good beach views. No issues, close to everything it's a Don&ShonaEats recommended place to stay.
Lady who checked us in was awesome and room perfect will definitely be back

The hotel has a fantastic view on Surfers Paradise and the beach, with comfy beds and spacious rooms. Breakfast Buffet offers basically anything you need or might like, make it really worth the money you are paying for it. Staff is so kind and attentive, making sure everything is filled up before finishing. Even got my own omlete, Chef Kane was really  helpful.

Stayed recently at Mantra on View Hotel and was very disappointed. The hotel is extremely dated and the photos online do not reflect the current condition of the property.

The noise from the neighbouring beach club in the original room was ridiculous. While the hotel may describe it as “lively,” this seriously downplays the reality. The noise level was excessive and the staff were clearly already aware of the issue. I was ultimately moved to the other side of the hotel, which resolved it, but guests should be clearly warned that this is far more than general atmosphere or background noise.

While renovations may be planned, in its current state it does not meet the expectations associated with booking through Accor. I hope the listing photos and guest disclosure around the beach club noise are reviewed so future travellers can make an informed decision.

I would recommend looking at other accommodation options.

We had a great time at Mantra view. Contrary to most negative opinion here we really enjoyed our stay. The hotel lives up the 4 star experience and the usual excellent accor hospitality. Room vmwas clean and spacious and the staff were very kind. We would mentioned by name Jackson at the breakfast check-in and Kane the Chef but really everyone was nice and professional. We would definitelly come back next time

I’m quite disappointed with my stay. I was there for 5 days and 4 nights, and the experience fell far below expectations.

Upon entering the room, I noticed stains and even pubic hair on the bedsheets, which was extremely off-putting. I requested a room change, but the receptionist said the only options were to remain in the same room, move to a lower-level room next to a bar (which would be noisy), or have the room cleaned. None of these felt like an acceptable solution.

The cleanliness overall was poor. The floor was dirty, and several smaller areas clearly hadn’t been cleaned properly. There was also hair in the closet, and the room itself looked quite run down, with visible damage on the walls and doors, as well as cracks in certain areas. It’s clear the property is in need of refurbishment.

Basic amenities were also lacking. No toothbrush or standard toiletries were provided. The hair dryer was very weak, although towels were provided. There was a kettle in the room, but we didn’t use it.

Given the condition of the room, I didn’t bother checking out the gym or spa, as it didn’t inspire confidence that those facilities would be well-maintained either. Also, the rooms weren’t soundproof. I could literally hear the conversations going on in other room.

Overall, this stay was disappointing and not up to standard

Chef Kane delivers an exceptional breakfast experience that truly stands out. His attention to detail, creativity, and passion for food are evident in every dish. The flavors are perfectly balanced, and each plate is presented beautifully, showing real artistry in the kitchen.

Staying at the Mantra Crown Towers and visited the buffet breakfast at the Mantra on the View. Service was lovely and the staff were welcoming and accommodating. Host Jackson was very welcoming and friendly upon arrival and chief Kane made me the best omelet. Definitely worth the visit, my two boys loved the experience and food as well ☺️

Excellent location! It is right in the heart of Surfers Paradise, offering stunning views. Staff is friendly and attentive. I even got a bottle of wine complimented as my birthday is coming up. Definitely worth staying here.

This was a very convenient location and the staff were very accommodating, we arrived 4 hours before check in and we greeted warmly and given a secure area to leave our luggage prior to check in. The lady on reception called me an hour after bag drop to inform us we could check in 3 hours earlier as our room was ready. Only downside was no fans in bathroom . 2 minute walk from beach in the heart of everything with foxtel on television we watched an afl game and a couple of movies also
Value for money
All around decent hotel for the price and location. Only reason I’m giving 4 stars is for no pool and odd parking setup. Otherwise ok.

I recently stayed at Mantra on View Hotel and unfortunately the experience was extremely disappointing.||The photos shown online do not reflect the current condition of the hotel. The property is very dated and clearly in need of significant renovation. While it appears that some refurbishment work may be underway, the overall state of the hotel feels tired and far below what you would expect when booking through Accor.||One of the biggest issues was the noise from the nearby beach club, which was extremely loud and continued late into the night. Staff seemed very aware that this is an ongoing problem affecting guests, which made it even more surprising that this is not clearly disclosed to people when booking.||Overall, the presentation of the property online feels misleading compared to the reality of the stay. Until the hotel has been fully renovated and the noise issues addressed, I would strongly recommend that travellers consider alternative accommodation.||The refund process following my complaint was also delayed beyond the timeframe originally advised, which added further frustration to the experience.||I hope this review helps other travellers make an informed decision.

Review of Room 1206 – Level 12, Surfers Paradise, Gold Coast, Queensland||It is important to understand that some rooms on Level 12 are privately owned apartments managed by individual owners or third-party managers rather than the hotel itself.||Unfortunately, Room 1206 — which I booked — was in an appalling and unacceptable condition. The apartment had a strong, persistent foul odor, was poorly cleaned, and contained furniture visibly damaged and swollen from moisture. There were also signs of liquid leakage and staining along the walls, which raised serious concerns about hygiene and overall safety.||I immediately contacted the managing representative to report these issues. While he initially promised to look into the matter the following day, once I clearly stated that it was impossible to stay in such conditions, he simply stopped responding, switched off his phone, and made no further attempt to resolve the situation.||Equally disappointing was the response from Agoda.com, the booking platform facilitating this listing. Despite providing evidence and clearly outlining the severity of the situation, the platform refused to properly investigate the complaint and ultimately sided with the property manager.||This experience reflects a serious lack of accountability and transparency. I strongly advise future guests to exercise extreme caution when booking privately managed apartments within this property, particularly on Level 12.

Overall run down location, hallways smelled of marijuana. I made the mistake of leaving medication on the bedside cabinet, for adhd, and it went missing.
The staff were asked "no one took it" was the response.
Was offered no compensation and had to chase it up 2ce.
It's a stimulant medication, and was taken the only day the rooms were serviced.
Will be reaching out to corporate about this.

Room 1206 – Do Not Book

I attempted to stay in Room 1206 at Mantra on View Hotel, Surfers Paradise, on 9 March 2026.

The room was in a condition that can only be described as unfit for accommodation. It fell far below any acceptable standard and did not resemble what was advertised in any meaningful way.

On arrival, the issues were immediately obvious and serious enough that I refused to stay. I had to secure alternative accommodation at my own expense.

I reported everything straight away to the unit manager and through Agoda, supported by photo and video evidence. Despite this, no proper action was taken. No replacement room was provided and no refund was issued. The response was dismissive and demonstrated a clear lack of responsibility.

This is not a minor issue or a matter of expectations — the room was simply not in a rentable condition.

It appears this unit is privately managed, and there is no reliable quality control. Booking this specific room carries a real risk of encountering unacceptable conditions.

I strongly advise avoiding Room 1206 entirely.

If you still choose to book, inspect immediately on arrival and be prepared to leave.

We have been staying at Mantra for 10 days and the buffet breakfast has been exceptional. A special shout out to Chef Lex who went above and beyond everytime he saw us. If he could, he would!! Grilled tomatoes? Mushrooms? Poached eggs? Smashed avo? It was never a problem and he would deliver it with a smile! Thanks Lex!

I booked this room specifically because it advertised free cancellation until mid-April, which was important as I don’t live in the state. It was also $150–$200 more expensive than other options, but I chose it for the flexibility.

A couple of weeks later, I needed to cancel as I’m unable to fly. When I went to cancel, I was shocked to see a cancellation fee for the full $1,014, even though I believed I was still within the free cancellation period.

I tried contacting the property multiple times through the number provided but received no response. Hours later, I got a notification that they attempted to take the full amount from my account without resolving anything or responding to my messages.

When I finally spoke to someone, I was told to contact Booking.com instead. Booking.com asked me to submit a request for free cancellation and explain my situation, which I did—but after more than 24 hours, there was still no response from the property.

Overall, the lack of communication and attempting to charge the full amount without clarification has been really disappointing. I wouldn’t recommend booking here based on this experience

Initiali was given a room which faced the disco pool,super noisy at nite.they oni stop ard 1am, I couldn't sleep & had a bad headache the next morning.luckily they managed to help me to change room to the other side after my feedback. The hotel location was a short walk to the beach & paradise center where all the food is.it will be good if the carpark fees are complementary for hotel stays.

Probably the worst hotel I've ever been to dirty inside manager was very rude and a week after we left they took $120 out my bank when I called them they said it was for maintenance but couldn't tell me what I understand paying for any damage but we damaged nothing why am I paying to maintain that place  it needs knocking down and it's definitely not my job to maintain it basically I was robbed also had something stolen from my room  I was completely screwed over

Its best feature is its location, steps from the beach. However, it's a hotel that has been downgraded; some rooms and hallways are in dire need of maintenance. I didn't realise the pool area no longer belonged to the hotel, which was a disappointment. I feel the price is high compared to other places with more amenities and more modern facilities. Breakfast was average as well.

Had bond returned then a week later took 120 out my account was told by the manager it was for maintenance  lol why am I paying for maintenance if it was damages which it wasn't we left the room as found i would understand but since when does the customer maintain the hotel and the manager Liam was the rudest man ive ever come accross

Unclean rooms, rude manager (Liam) , got told i could get no help with bags as he was the only one working because everybody except him was on lunch and lets just say for two hrs. I was so stressed out i even got teary. Not good enough! After releasing my bond the next day took extra money out so called hotel and he had left for holidays. Ha |I had to pay $40 late checkout until someone could help me as im. Single parent with autistic child. Just wow
كانت الغرفه جميلة جداً والفندق موقعه ممتاز جداً

入住的當天晚上九點多要從地下室停車場上樓的時候發現門把鎖壞掉導致我困在停車場也沒有感應卡可以開門，後來我是走車道上到大廳才有辦法離開停車場，要退房的當天是10點退房，9:30的時候我還在房內整理行李就有房務人員不太有禮貌的敲我的房門開門後直接問我什麼時候要離開我跟他說五到10分鐘後離開，然後他們就在我的門口一直等著我離開讓我覺得很有壓力，隨後我就被迫check out讓他們可以打掃，除了房務人員以外，其他的櫃檯人員都蠻友善的。
food was wonderful, staff was very kind to us, we'll be back definitely

Dear Mantra on View Team,||I recently stayed at your hotel for one week, and I had a truly wonderful experience.||The location is absolutely perfect—just a short 3-minute walk to the beach, with so many restaurants, grocery stores, and a liquor shop nearby. Being close to the tram station made it very easy to get around, and I also loved that the hotel is right next to Cali Beach Club, which added to the vibrant atmosphere of the area.||One of the highlights of my stay was the beautiful ocean view from my balcony. Waking up to that view every day was simply amazing.||I would also like to thank your friendly and helpful staff for their excellent service. I especially appreciate that my hair straightener was safely returned—this level of honesty and care truly made a difference.||The hotel was clean and comfortable, and there were so many activities nearby, including golf and shopping centres, which made my stay even more enjoyable.||Overall, I had a fantastic time and would definitely stay again. Thank you for such a memorable experience.||Kind regards,  |Akira Pawika

Malfunctioning Arcade Machines & Zero Responsibility from Hotel Staff

I am writing this to warn others about the Game Zone on the ground floor of Mantra On View. On March 16, I lost money to multiple faulty machines:
1. Change Machine: I inserted a $5 bill, but the machine only recognized it as $1 and dispensed only 3 coins. It essentially swallowed $4. (As shown in the photo, it only registered $1).
2. Soccer Game Machine: Swallowed several 20c coins. No game started, and the refund button didn't work.
3. Air Hockey Machine: Swallowed a coin and failed to operate.
When I reported this to the hotel staff, they were incredibly dismissive, stating it wasn't their responsibility and telling me to contact the game company myself. These machines are located inside the hotel premises; therefore, the hotel should ensure they are functional or facilitate immediate refunds. This experience completely ruined my holiday mood. I demand a proper refund and a professional response regarding this poor management.

Had a great 5 night stay , clean friendly hotel , excellent breakfast with a good variety of nice fresh food , close to cypress tram stop and only a 50 cent fare from Coolangatta airport to hotel (777 bus to Broadbeach then tram to cypress avenue )

私は日本語しか話せませんが、フロントの若い男性に親切にしていただき、とても助かりました。おかげでオプショナルツアーにスムーズに参加することができ、本当に感謝しています。
3連泊しましたが、部屋もずっときれいにしていただき快適でした。
またぜひ泊まりたいし、人におすすめしたいです。
どうもありがとうございました。
Outdated but in a great location, beds are okay and bathroom is big. Needs renovations but worth it for the price we paid

The hotel itself is great the rooms are nice, the location is fantastic, and overall the property is very comfortable. Unfortunately our experience trying to extend our stay was extremely frustrating due to poor communication and lack of clarity from the staff.

When we first asked to extend our booking, we were initially told we would need to change rooms and that it couldn’t be fixed. Then we were told to pay for the extension online, and after doing that we were asked to call the head office reservations team to sort it out. The whole process became confusing and disjointed. The call dropped out, no one called us back, and we couldn’t get through again.

The next day I went downstairs to the desk to try and sort things out in person, only to be told we could pay an extra $30 to move back to a beach view, because apparently the system had changed our booking to a city view.

Then on the third morning, when we went down for breakfast before checking out, we were told breakfast wasn’t included, even though we asked to extend our existing booking under the same terms which had been included for the first two nights.

After all the confusion and the fact we had spent over $900 staying here, it would have been nice if someone had shown a bit of goodwill even something small like a complimentary coffee, but instead we were just told “sorry, we can’t help you.”

Currently in our room. Nightclub below the balcony, music blasting since lunchtime. Bring earplugs!! Otherwise location is great, room is comfy, beds are fine. Luckily I like the music type being played but if you don’t like club tracks stay somewhere away from here. If you do then it’s absolutely fine 😁
バスタブにカビが生えていたのが残念でした。ホテルからの景色、サーファーズパラダイスまでの距離の近さは大変良かったです。

Fantastic 2 night stay
Comfy bed, invigorating shower, lovely views

Great location, but hotel in general is really aging.  they've lost their pool and hot tubs, and the common areas and rooms were very simple.  Construction equipment out the window, and a loud nightclub.  Location is still good, but it's not what it used to be....

Stayed at Mantra on View (room 912) and was really disappointed compared to my stay about three years ago. The rooms used to feel warm and cozy with the original heavy furniture and darker tones, but the recent renovation feels very cheap and clinical. The laminate flooring already looks poorly installed with visible gaps, the walls are painted bright white like a hospital, and the lighting doesn’t even match (one warm, one bright white).

They’ve also added a small kitchenette that feels unnecessary, replacing the nice dark table the room used to have. The black bed runner looks unsanitary compared to the usual white hotel linen, and the beds are so light now that they slide across the floor when you sit on them, probably because of the laminate instead of carpet.

Overall the renovation feels rushed and tacky, and unfortunately the hotel has lost the cozy atmosphere it had a few years ago.
Great location and convenient facilities.

Having seminar with them. Outstanding service for event.

Room is clean, and thoughtful conversation and assistance.

Arrived in our room to find tv out dated,  bath tub not functioning and a very ordinary small room  and no pools which was a surprise as the website pics showed multiple pools   when we were given a second room  the TV was still difficult to use  however the view was good  and the free breakfast ,for the stuff around was appreciated ,all in all dissapointing  staff were difficult to deal with as we were complaining bout our rooms   maintenance man was great and  let me know owners just won't spend money on improving things ,   very frustrated  sorry to say .Honest but harsh I'm sorry

The hotel itself is beautiful and well situated in the area, close to all amenities.
However, the few members of staff on reception I came across were nothing but unhelpful. My key card wouldn't open the gym, they were of no help whatsoever. Every single hotel in the world allows bags to be left on the day of departure for a period of time, not here.
Great location and clean rooms

We enjoyed our stay very much.
We were disappointed that there was not a pool on site, Cali Beach is very close but seemed the wrong environment. Other than that all good.
Nice view 👌
とても良かったですが、金庫が使えませんでした。

Security deposit still not returned.

I am still waiting for the refund of the deposit, as you promised.
Is very touching, that you responded on my google review, but your are still not being nice to your clients.
Feeling sorry does not change a single thing, since you are not trustwirthy!
Please, make a return, as I left everything intact!
Great location Clean worth adding for a stay
Nice accommodation, good location. Only downside was no restaurant/ bar open in-house on a Thursday night when it was pouring with rain

I had a very disappointing experience at this hotel. The room was dirty and I even saw cockroaches, which made it feel extremely unhygienic and uncomfortable. The customer service was poor and unhelpful, especially when I asked for sanitary pads for my partner at 11 pm and was told none were available — such a basic necessity should be accessible.

On top of that, loud music from next door went on for hours and made it impossible to sleep. For the price paid, I expected at least basic cleanliness and amenities. I would not recommend staying here — save your money and look elsewhere.

Mantra on View used to be one of my favourite places to stay
Because of the hotel's greed, doubling the room rates, I no longer feel that way
There's a lot of maintenance that needs addressing
If you have to park your vehicle in the public carpark next door, then you do so at your own risk. Got slugged $20 by the Mantra. Witnessed vehicles with smashed in windows. Quite unnerving
22층뷰...지립니더

We are booked for our anniversary getaway for 2 nights. Well the exterior of the hotel looks nice and fact that our last stay with Mantra( towers) was nice, we paid more for a good view. There is great view , just that it. Rest of other sucks.

Firstly, there is no any connection between the parking and hotel(lifts). You have to walk up the stairs with your luggage to next floor to reach the lift. Secondly, room that I paid extra is quite old, old furniture, old layout, tables inside are wobbly.

Next, no pool. You are in Surfers and without pool.
Also, we had bed and Breakfast booked, so we went for Breakfast. Layout; just for the sec of they showing that they have breakfast. No too many options for food. Eat what is displayed. We wanted to get Omlet, staffs were busy and could not find one. Scrambled egg was soggy.

So with this we went to talk with reception early in the morning just after breakfast about cancelling the booking as we didnt want to ruin rest of our holiday, well the first lay was about to do it, but another manager stepped in and say  ‘no matter you stay or not we cannot refund you’ . They would be charging us though we checked out.

Well, we are stuck here on our anniversary with a shitty room(not complaining the view) shitty hotel and shitty service.

Never recommend anyone for this hotel.

Worst Accor Stay – Not Family Friendly Due to Extreme Night Noise
We are a family of three (with a 4-year-old child) staying four nights between Christmas and New Year. This review is being written on our second night, close to midnight, as sleep has been impossible.
One entire side of the hotel directly faces the Cali Beach open-air nightclub. Despite being on the 18th floor with windows fully closed, the music is extremely loud and continues until the early hours of the morning. This is not occasional bass noise — full songs can be clearly heard throughout the night INCLUDING PEOPLE AT THE CLUB SINGING IT.
There was no warning at the time of booking about this level of noise. Under these conditions, this property should not be marketed as family- or child-friendly.
When we raised the issue with reception yesterday, the only solution offered was earplugs. Unfortunately, these are ineffective for this level of noise and not suitable for a young child.
In addition, room cleanliness was below expectations. No drinking water was provided, the kettle appeared dated and unhygienic, the hair dryer had hair and lint stuck to it, and the bathtub does not drain completely, which could be a safety concern.
I regularly recommend Accor properties to friends and family and generally have very positive experiences across the group. This stay, however, has been extremely disappointing, especially given the price paid. Sadly, this has been our worst experience at an Accor property, and I would not recommend this hotel, particularly to families or anyone expecting a restful stay.

I have written to the hotel directly, awaiting a response but this has spoilt our holidays quite a bit. The minimum anyone hopes for after a long day on a holiday with family is a quite night of sleep, which has been hard to get for the last two days.

Staying 4 nights between Christmas and New Year. This review is written on our (family of three) 2nd night just before midnight. This has to be our WORST EXPERIENCE in an Accor hotel.
One side of the entire hotel faces the Cali Beach night club (on open air club) in Surfers Paradise and even through closed windows we can hear loud music blaring until wee hours of the morning AND we are on 18th floor. I’m not talking about few heavy bass beats, you can lip sync entire songs through the night.
There is NO warning on the hotel website at the time of booking either. THIS SHOULD NOT BE LISTED AS A CHILD/FAMILY FRIENDLY HOTEL. I get it’s a busy period between Christmas and New Year and changing room is not as straight forward but if the hotel front desk has to OFFER EAR PLUGS to get a quiet night’s sleep inside a fully priced hotel room on a high floor… I don’t know what else to say..I’ll never recommend this place to anyone. We (two adults and one child) had an absolutely unacceptable and infuriating experience staying in Room 709 at Mantra on View in December 2025.

The room was filthy. The air-conditioning vent was clogged with dust, and after just one night it made all three of us sick. The carpet reeked and the smell was so strong at night it felt suffocating to breathe in that room. We couldn’t even open the door or windows for relief because it was too hot outside.

The worst part is what this did to my child. My son is asthmatic, and he spent almost the entire night coughing, forcing us to keep him on his asthma plan for a large part of the night. Meanwhile, my wife and I woke up with sore throats from the dust and the disgusting carpet. This wasn’t a “minor inconvenience” — it was a health hazard.

And it didn’t stop there. This disastrous one-night stay wrecked the rest of our holiday. All three of us suffered a serious allergic reaction — sore throat, fever, sneezing, and spasmodic coughing — which ruined the vacation we had planned for the following days.

On top of the hygiene issues, the room itself was dated and disappointing. The “entertainment system” is embarrassingly outdated — no smart TV, just backward, old equipment. The room and facilities look worn out, including the fittings, and clearly haven’t been properly maintained.

I’m an Accor Gold member, and this is my second worst experience at an Accor property on the Gold Coast. Frankly, this feels like a pattern — the Novotel Surfers Paradise was also filthy, old, worn out, and poorly maintained.

With this experience, how do you expect one to choose to stay an Accor hotel?

Breakfast was really good! Lots to choose from.

Great location but that’s it. Gym was closed, rooms were smelly, dirty and dusty. There is no pool and the young staff were friendly but the manager refused to refund more than one night of our 4 night stay. We ended up booking elsewhere to save the trip being ruined. Do not stay here. EVER
Great place to stay and convenient location
Terrible services and super dirty place bathroom was terrible the party in front to the big party is no the place for go to have a relax holidays

My go-to hotel when staying in Surfers Paradise, won’t find better value. Amazing view of the beach and city, plenty of amenities ( plunge pools/gym/tanning beds etc). Staff are always friendly and room service/housekeeping is great.

The staff, including the cleaning staff, were all very friendly and helpful.  The hotel is definitely showing its age.  The doors all need to be jiggled to open and the lift often takes a while to recognise the keycard.  There are no washing machines which is fine, but having no clothes horses or line, other than the one in the damp bathroom, to dry towels etc on is a bit tricky, especially since they are 20 seconds from the beach.  Buffet breakfast was fantastic, so many options, and my daughter loved the self-serve pancake machine with maple syrup.

I thoroughly enjoyed my stay at Mantra on View which has magnificent balcony views of the Gold Coast and the beach. The amenities were superb and the staff were very friendly, courteous and professional. I highly recommend this wonderful hotel!
Mold all over the room. Seems like the haven’t cleaned it in years. Didn’t change our room had to find somewhere else. Owner wasn’t helpful
Still waiting on welcoming drink from reception. But did get bottle of wine in room. Did say a drink as well from reception but no where to get it from.
Easy to check in, convenient to so much in Surfers paradise, restaurants, shopping, and transport.

We stayed here recently and unfortunately found the experience quite disappointing. The hotel itself feels very dated and could really use a refurbishment. Our room wasn’t cleaned at all during our stay, which was disappointing.

The car park was another frustration, as there’s no lift to the lobby — only stairs — and this wasn’t mentioned to us at check-in. It would have been helpful to know beforehand, especially when carrying luggage.

Our room also overlooked Cali Beach Club, and the music was so loud it sounded like it was playing right inside the room until around 12–1am, making it very difficult to sleep (even as high up as the 18th flood).

There was also an issue with our bill at checkout, though to the hotel’s credit, this was resolved quickly.

We had a complimentary bottle of wine included in our booking, but it was forgotten and only provided after we went down to reception to ask about it.

Overall, while some of the staff were helpful and polite, the stay just didn’t meet expectations — especially given the noise, lack of cleaning, and dated facilities.

This place is so outdated. It smells in the rooms. The quality of the breakfast buffet is poor.  The toaster doesn’t even work properly. The glass container are not clean. Not a good selection of food for breakfast

Check-in was straightforward, except for one confusing issue that the hotel no longer accepts cash for the room deposit, even though the form still lists cash as an option.

Upon entering our room, we noticed what appeared to be blood smudges on the wall near the bathroom light switch. We attempted to contact reception four times with no response, so my partner ended up cleaning it himself.

When we went to place drinks in the fridge, we discovered a half-eaten acai bowl left inside. Again, we tried calling reception from both the room phone and my mobile, around ten times in total, but no one answered. We eventually went downstairs to inform reception in person. The staff’s response was rather dismissive, though they did later arrange for a deep clean of the room.

My concern is that these issues should never have occurred in the first place. Proper cleaning and room checks are basic expectations for any guest. While a bottle of wine was left in the room afterward, it hardly compensates for the unsanitary and disappointing experience.

Unfortunately, based on this stay, I would not return and would not recommend this property to others.
Very nice, close to the beach and only a stroll from Cavill Av👍
Room standards are very poor.
The rooms were horrendous, would in the bathroom rising damp in the wardrobe and a musty smell in the room. Location was amazing but couldn’t take away from how bad the room was
Easy to find great atmosphere and comfortable.
The view from our balcony was breathtaking!

Customer Courtesy - Zero
Hygiene - Zero

Expectation is more from brand like Mantra. It was unpleasant experience.

Hotel from the hell.
No idea how do they call it a business. Never picks a call for any kind of inquiries.
One staff running reservation, cleaning, accounting. Waited at the lobby more than an hour just to collect key after standard C/I time.
They should go bankrupt.
Great view friendly staff

it was a decenct stay i would say. but i was expecting a lot more, such as a promised ocean view (but in reality it wasnt really ocean view) and we stayed a total of 4 days 3 nights. 2 days we didnt get housekeeping. altough after the first miss we told the reception asking for housekeeping, when we came back the next day, it was still undone. basically thats it. everything else was mid. just the housekeeping part.
床很舒服位置舒適

I had recently returned from my holiday from surfers and stayed at Mantra on view hotel. Upon checking in I paid for the bond then proceeded to my room.
First impression was disappointing as it was not how it was perceived online. The shower pressure was next to none, the hallways were outdated, the rooms were alright but I wouldn't recommend staying here.
It's now been 2 weeks since I've checked out but there was never a release on my bond money. I've been contacting the hotel consistently although have been ignored, also sent numerous emails but no response was initiated. I've contacted the bank and they say it's the hotel that needs to fix it.
The only good thing that came out of this place was the location.
Clean, Mercury present, however untaunted via the fire safety systems which was nice, I'll give it that!
I just feel this hotel needs serious work. Bathroom was full of mould .

The property is quite dated and feels more like a motel than a hotel. Unfortunately, the Wi-Fi wasn’t working, and the air-conditioning was dusty and not very effective, which made the room a bit uncomfortable. The overall décor feels tired and could use some refreshing. On a positive note, the location is very convenient and close to key attractions. With some updates and maintenance, this place could be much better.
Well priced and good facilities..located within walking distance to shops

Room space, cleanliness etc. was good. However I needed for 2 pax but I had to book 2 double beds. Location is wonderful to enjoy beach,  city center etc. However basic facilities like toothbrush etc. was paid.

The two concerns we had were minor but a shame,
1st was the building next door was having a weekend pool party on the roof some kind of night club thing, was loud and didn't stop until 12. Not the Mantra's fault but still a shame.

The 2nd was that for some reason pest control spraying was scheduled for the time we were in the room, we didn't make a fuss and it wasn't the pest control persons fault but could've been done before or after we were staying in the room. Was a disappointing experience which we didn't want to address at the time due to not wanting to sour the mood of the stay.

Otherwise location and hotel itself were good (our 2nd time at this Mantra) hence why we came back, not sure we'd consider a 3rd :(

Paid for sea view could barely see it on the corner of the small balcony.
After paying for the hotel they later advice that we have to pay fee for cleaning but they only cleaned the room once they  didn’t provide any cleaning service or amenities
The Wi-Fi was not working on our stay, even the room was  old school and the bathroom was old and had an foul smell complained to the host he was extremely rude and waste of time didn’t do anything about the matters even tried to accuse us of lying which was not fair even the cleaning staff came in our room earlier morning when we were sleeping he opened the door with his key card invading our privacy and was rude
I came here for a family holiday but couldn’t really enjoy our stay
I would not recommend anyone staying here and experiencing such a horrible experience like we did !!
Lovely staff, great location. Would definitley stay again.

It was an ok stay - a few staff members I encountered were decent. Asked to have membership number added at check in yet still wasn’t done and had to end up reaching out to membership team via enquiry form who managed to sort it a week later.
The rooms are super outdated, yet everything needed work and the view was decent (partial ocean view) but the Location is great though - keep in mind you get what you pay for. We have stayed at a number of different hotels so we knew it wouldn’t be like hotels we have stayed at before (eg. Sheraton, Hilton, JW Marriott)

We were very disappointed in the condition of the rooms. Our initial room had dirty towel and bath on our arrival. When speaking to management we were moved to another room which was also very much in need of renovation. We were given free breakfast for our 2 nights which was appreciated. Obviously management can only work with what they have but this is definitely not a good look for Mantra.

I haven’t stayed in such a dirty hotel for a long time. When we entered the room, the carpet was extremely dirty and looked like it hadn’t been cleaned in years.
The edges of the bed were also stained, and there were wipe marks on the bed surface. The ventilation shaft in the bathroom was dusty as well. It’s hard to call this a 4-star hotel, it felt more like a 2-star one. In addition, it was quite a shock that on the third day they came into our room to do pest control… Very unpleasant!!  The hotel should put more effort into cleanliness!

The room was basic but comfortable, it was unfortunately not very clean when we arrived but someone was sent to clean it again and we were offered free breakfast one morning as compensation. The breakfast buffet was decent but we opted not to pay to return again during our stay. We also had to request additional blankets as we were only given very thin ones and one spare ( for 2 beds). The room was a little dated but had the basic essentials, microwave was down stairs in the lobby though which was a bit impractical. The staff were helpful and friendly and we were close enough to walk to most of our locations. Parking was pricey at $20 a day. Overall it was good basic accommodation.

“I was looking for a place to stay in the Gold Coast for two nights — most hotels were really expensive, so this one seemed relatively affordable. The hotel has a really good location, right in the center of Surfers Paradise. We booked a city-view room, but it actually came with a little glimpse of the ocean as well — quite a nice surprise!

Parking isn’t free, but it’s cheaper than normal. After paying at the front desk, you’ll receive a PIN code that allows you to access the parking lot multiple times, which is really convenient.

The hotel itself feels a bit old-fashioned, but the staff are very warm and welcoming, which makes up for it.
No wifi and told them three times and no fix.
Great location central to everything. Room was outdated and not the cleanest had mould in the bathroom .

No Pool.  Gym and other features closed off for renovations.  Bathroom tub is rusty and damaged, balcony is tiny, not as pictured in most rooms.  Double beds, not queen beds.  No coffee maker, just a kettle.  No parking included and none nearby suitable for 1.9m or over vehicles.  'Private' bathroom has a glass window into bedrooms.  Loud club "The Beach" playing until late and facing balcony door is not close to soundproof.  Poor beach views.
That was so terrible. Totally different was what showed on photos.

We stayed in a level 5 in Mantra on View during the long holiday. The location was amazing, needless to say we can reached surfers paradise by short walking and traveled anywhere around GC due to its short distance to tram stops. The room was enough for 4 of us (parent with 2 teenagers). Strength point from me: the lemongrass soap, shampoo, lotions were the best! They also went extra miles with various breakfast in level 2.
Downside: I have to pay parking for extra $20, while the email mentioned it just $15.

Mantra on view is a bit of a mix bag.

I did enjoy our stay and I think they did their loyalty program exceptionally well.

So I'll start off with the positives.

I really enjoyed the entire check-in procedure the fact that as platinum members you were extended all types of courtesy. This has been above and beyond most other places I've stayed at for platinum which was very nice.

Simple example is just having a small area called an off for the platinum members to use during breakfast. Visually not that appealing but conceptually wonderful I do like to be taken care of.

The only gripe that I have is that the rooms need a little bit of refurbishment mattress is probably due for a replacement.

However overall really do not have any complaints I would gladly come back.

And the reason I come back is mostly because of service and how well they have treated their loyal members.

Below average rooms, dated facilities.
one person at reception at check in and check out times. The cards to the parking didn’t work.

Even though staff were friendly.  no drinking water bottles provided in rooms. People were getting water from local store. Breakfast was okay. No dinner service provided in the hotel except only Fridays. For families with children no pool is available at least another six month as it’s under construction as we told after I asked at the reception then I asked what other options were available we’ve been told to ask other hotels pool facilities. Overall it was just for bed & breakfast. We booked the room ocean view and we end up staying in city view room which we weren’t able to sleep or sit at the balcony bc of the building construction projects and roadworks.

Check in 2 room wasn’t ready had to wait 45 mins. Had ocean view room . Rooms average tiny tv away from bed so u can’t see it bathroom hair and yuck on floor . No swimming pool area . If you need sleep avoid place at all cost Callie beach night club next to ocean view rooms noisy , loud glad we wasted money on this crappy place!!!!! The only good thing was the breakfast. AVOID THIS PLACE !!!!
如果你们的客房以及卫生间能够与你们的大堂装修相符合，就会感到非常好

Mantra on View Hotel is an ideal place for someone who is looking for somewhere which is in a great location and only needs the hotel to sleep at night. The location couldn't be better.

However, this hotel isn't perfect. First of all, there was advertised that there was a spa but it was under 'maintenance', making it really boring while staying in the hotel. Room service was unpredictable, and was sometimes done sloppy and rushed or completely missed.

The rooms themselves were pretty good despite hot having a kitchen. However, the glass of the shower was to small, hence after anyone took a shower, the whole bathroom would get absolutely soaked. The balcony could've been bigger.

The location is amazing as it is close to the beach and all the restaurants, but it is right next to some noisy neighbours who sometimes make it a bit difficult at night to sleep.

The 'view' on this hotel is definitely 3 stars, as my room was facing down on the spa, the one that needed 'maintenance' and they weren't even doing anything there. We could still slightly see the beach and the 'slingshot' which was very cool to see at night. I would recommend this hotel.
看上去高大尚，结果客房非常简陋，我们还是提前两个月预定的，这个酒店应该是在当地非常有名的连锁品牌，可能经常接待大量的团队，所以整体上不值得，而且价格不便宜，这种价格如果是在比较规模小一点的酒店会非常好吧。尤其是卫生间太陈旧了，还有停车场和大堂之间没有电梯，非常恐怖，年纪大的根本上不了，可以看我发的照片，停车场还要20澳币一天，预定房间的时候根本没有说明，

You are booking this hotel for its location, nothing else.
Amenities are aged. No kettle in the room. No slippers provided. No welcome drinks, not even bottled water. Room is poorly lit, bedside lamps are the only light source in the room, good thing is that they are bright enough. Bad thing is that they are too bright to be next to the bed! There are not even power outlets near the bed so you can’t even charge your phone next to you. Shower and bathtub are also very old.
Worst thing of all, the reception is extremely under staffed. We checked in at around 8pm on a Friday and had to wait in line for almost an hour to be checked in. There are 3 reception desks with only 1 staff. It feels like this hotel is not being taken care of and managed properly.
EDIT: this hotel also made me pay $200AUD at check in for a deposit, which is fine, many hotels do this. But most hotels would refund the deposit at checkout. It’s been a couple business days after I checked out and I still do not see the refund. I do not know how long it’s going to take for the refund to arrive. This is ridiculous. I don’t understand why they need to hold my deposit for so long!?

Phòng ốc sạch...
Vị trí thuận tiện, view đẹp...

Location is the only thing that’s good in this place. Service isn’t too bad. But I’d say it is not the best place for family. The room is too small. Although the sunrise looks good and the city scape at night looks beautiful too. Cleanliness is OK. I just noticed that the lobby doesn’t look welcoming at all, it just feels empty. There’s no elevator all the way to the basement parking lot. You have to carry your luggage up a steep staircase. I wish the reception lady told us all about this so we could have unloaded the luggage before heading to the car park. Parking charge is $20 per day. Poor amenities.
It was clean, and spacious, best view of everything and staff friendly. Recommend the breakfast!
Terrible they made me wait 3 hours just to get into a room and were very rude and were terrible at explaining things

My friend and I stayed here for 4 days rest. We looked forward to breakfast every morning. The amazing Chef Robert made us the best omelettes every morning, it really started our day off well. Thank you Robert, keep doing a great job. 😀

Long queue at the reception, no staff around to ask around and only choice is to queue at the reception for a short inquiry...there should be someone at the concierge desk to assist guests and handle bags. room is pretty old, needs a lot of refurbishment

Never stayed in the property and was trying to call them never answered because I was trying to cancel my reservation and didn’t let me and then I had a transfer from my bank to them to stay and I asked them on the day for a refund and they said they will get back to me asap and never called so I tried it on booking.com app because I booked one there wasn’t able to get a refund I booked it for the 17 to 18 September and they now say they can’t get me a refund don’t recommend to stay there and rude managers and staff

I would never recommend this hotel to anyone. We are still here with a week left in our stay, and it has been nothing short of awful.
•	The Wi-Fi does not work at all, despite being advertised.
•	The bathroom is in shocking condition: no bath mat, no hand soap, and there were even stains left in the toilet on arrival.
•	The hallways are dirty, with rubbish left sitting around.
•	The hotel’s main selling points — the pool and spa facilities — have been closed without notice, yet they’re still advertised on the website. This is completely misleading.
•	The contact number provided at check-in doesn’t even connect, leaving us with no way to properly raise issues.

This is the most disappointing and misleading hotel experience I’ve ever had. If you’re considering staying here, save yourself the money and the stress go somewhere else.
My stay here was great! I had a lovely time with my family and the reception staff were very helpful! Thanks team
Excellent location, satisfying cleanliness, and friendly, helpful staff members.
Good hospitality and services provided during our stay, well located, cleanliness is awesome
If you look closer, everything is quite worn out with many cracks etc in the room. Felt it was dirty.Also, restaurants/bar only opens on weekends...
Great service, could do with some updating, disappointed that I was not told the spas were under renovation

We first stayed here back in 2006/2007. Unfortunately not much has changed since then. Other than a rebrand (now Mantra) and some badly applied paint to the room doors, I don't think much money has been injected into this hotel.

We did not venture into the restaurant nor the pool area ( as it was early Spring and the pool appeared to be full of leaves.

Upon arrival we waited what seemed like too long for one of the two front counter staff to serve us. The lady was helpful and friendly. We were offered parking at $20 for the night which we agreed to. Parking was via their delivery dock entrance, the parking area was a bit unclean and not very well lit in places. From there it was a walk up two flights of stairs to get back to the foyer level. We were told about this prior to parking which was good. This stairwell area is very dated, unclean and unloved.

Leaving the elevator and walking to our room, we notice the badly applied (needed a sand and undercoat) grey paint on all the room doors was scratched and peeling, flakes of paint on the floor suggested this traffic area doesn't get the cleaning it needs.

Unfortunately this 'feel' continued into our room where we found carpet with pulls, small stains on the bed covers and old, dated, and damaged built in shelving. There was a random green chair at the 'desk' but no lamp, so writing my memoirs would have to wait.

Balcony door didn't seem to open? Bathroom had a shower over bath which may not suit all travellers but did provide a warm shower. Toilet paper was soft!  Beds were fair, not too had but not up with modern standards.

Glad we were only there for the night and we booked this based on price and the fact we had been there before....much has changed in the last 18 years....fair enough but not good enough.

Nice enough place bed was comfy Nosh restaurant was nice, overall with a bit of a tidy up and maybe paint the bathroom ceilings it will be a lot better place to stay, it’s only a small thing but comments were made by a few people.
Chambre très propre et le personnel de ménage et d'accueil très professionnel
Propreté et literie à revoir.

Staff were caring and were helpful.
My only complaint was there were not enough luggage trolleys, and as I cannot lift anything weighty it was a bit of a problem.
在黄金海岸位置非常好的酒店，酒店我住的房间是能够直接看到海岸线的。唯一游客来说就是没有拖鞋比较不方便。
Great location, easy walk to the beach,Caville Ave and a number of restaurants .  Awesome breakfast!

The room was okay… wasn’t what I was expecting, quite dirty in some spots and the carpet had a smell to it along with the couch that was in the room, it smelt like someone had used it as a toilet.

People at reception were very friendly and helping but probably wouldn’t be staying there again.
Location was good, 2minutes from the beach and town centre.

The rooms are a disgrace old falling apart never been renovated. The staff are great,  when you walk in you think this looks nice, then you go up to the rooms and the truth is revealed. Doors and drawers falling apart, no microwave, at least the kettle was clean, everything is just well past it's used by date.
Yeah could not do enough for us and went above and beyond
$150 for a night in a hotel that’s perfectly on par in every way with $300+ stays that I’ve had in the past. Incredible value.

We had a wonderful stay at Mantra on View Surfers Paradise. The check-in process was very smooth, and all the staff were friendly and welcoming. Our room was spacious and well-equipped with all the amenities you could need, and the bed was one of the most comfortable I've slept in. The view from our balcony was beautiful.
The hotel's location is a huge plus. It's in an excellent spot with easy access to everything. The tram is just 50 meters away, making it incredibly convenient to get around. It was also great to have a chemist next door that's open late. The efficient staff and fantastic amenities made this a truly comfortable and enjoyable stay.
Excellent location and a very well maintained and secure property.
sound
Amazing views, reception smelled so Beautiful. Thank you

The location is perfect and the initial experience of the hotel is lovely at reception, but then you have to park in an underground basement and walk up a big flight of stairs to the lobby as there’s no elevator. The corridor after the stairs that you have to walk to get to the main hotel is dirty, smelt of urine and had graffiti on doors. Once we got to the lobby and up to our room we were also faced with dirty corridor walls. We got into our room and noticed the mould that covered the ceiling right above the shower. In the main room, the light fixture was broken and hanging at an angle and the couch was so worn out and didn’t even have pillows to try to make it look inviting. The tables and cabinets were chipped and the rest of the fixtures and walls looked like they were falling apart. The carpet had stains and the tv remote had snot on it. Overall the room was atrocious and not welcoming at all. We went to reception and asked for a refund on the parking, which was $60 for three nights, which was given to us and when we told them we were leaving to stay somewhere else they offered us a better room, which I don’t understand as to why we were given a poor room at a 4 star hotel if there were better options available. We didn’t trust that the “better” room would even be better, so we left and stayed at Harbour Quays apartments which was absolutely amazing and one of the best stays I’ve ever had.

整體滿意，地點極佳。
附近cole和woolworth超市走路10分鐘內可抵達，沙灘走路約7分鐘也可抵達，晚上可徒步去逛大街。
早餐種類蠻多的，連續吃了4天，還是覺得不錯。
房間夠大，但隔音稍稍差，海浪聲，走廊聲等，介意安靜者建議自備耳塞。

This was our third stay here in as many years, making it a convenient base for enjoying the Gold Coast while visiting extended family. We had excellent service from Alexia at the front desk, who promptly moved us to another room after we played her a recording of hammer-drill noise from renovations in the room directly above. We tolerated several hours of it on the first day (four days into our stay)—so loud we couldn’t hold a conversation or hear the TV—but a second straight day was too much.

A tip for future guests: bring a physical debit or credit card for the room bond! At check-in, the front desk couldn’t accept my digital card (and NAB doesn’t display full card numbers for security reasons), nor would they accept a cash deposit, which I gladly offered instead. Fortunately, after checking on my 83-year-old mother waiting in the foyer, she had ignored my advice to leave her purse at home and had her debit card and passport with her. Why didn’t I bring mine? Since our last visit, my driver’s licence, Medicare card, and bank cards have all gone digital—so I didn’t expect to need them, and a physical card wasn’t mentioned in our reservation email.

Despite these hiccups, we’ll be back for the cleanliness of the hotel and the convenience to the beach, shops, and tram.
Good view from the room. Near to tram station and restaurants.
위치가 정말 좋고 접근성이 좋습니다
Great stay, good value.

Our stay at this hotel was wonderful! The location is absolutely perfect – everything from shops to restaurants and activities is just a short walk away, with tram and bus stops conveniently nearby. We hardly needed to use Uber, which saved us a lot. Check-in was smooth and hassle-free, and the staff made us feel welcome right away. The highlight was the stunning ocean view from our room, which made the stay even more special. It’s the ideal place for both convenience and relaxation. We thoroughly enjoyed our time here and would definitely come back again on our next visit!
Well located.  Very comfortable. Staff friendly.
入住時職員自行加大了房間，無額外收費，所以住得挺舒適的。地理位置佳，到處遊覽、用餐也很方便。

Upon check-in, the person on reception should have suggested that we unload our luggage and leave it in the lobby before parking, seeing's how we were directed to park in the basement carpark and had to carry our bags up a huge flight of stairs in order to reach the lobby elevators. There needs to be TV instructions in rooms, as later that night we tried to use the TV remote and couldn't work out how to change channels. We reported it the next morning and were told it would be looked at. Got back on the second night and was the same. Someone came to the room and showed us the 'trick' to fix the issue. Friends of ours also staying there, told us they had the same problem. Also got back on the second night to a bathroom abandoned midway through the cleaning service...had to ask for toilet paper, tissues (tissue box holder left undone on bathroom bench) and we were also one towel short.
Overall, we were disappointed with this hotel's service. (While I was waiting to check in, I even overheard someone on reception asking a colleague about a customer trying to book an Ocean View Spa appointment & being told that "there's no spa in this hotel". I later walked past a banner advertising the Ocean View Spa! ) The rooms and bathrooms are also in need of renovation.

My friend reserved for the 2 of us. While she had to perform in the night, I wanted to be at the hotel earlier. But the reservation picture wasn't enough. They wouldn't let me in. I needed to wait for my friend in the lobby. Worst hotel
Always great to get back to the Mantra on View in Surfers. Cost competitive hotel with great service. Spacious rooms and great views of Surfers at night and beach.
Nice units great ocean views but no parking !!!
Great room good price shirt stroll to Cavell
Need some work done on the room, particularly the bathroom. Just look shabby, outdated and old. And how about a microwave? For the tariff charged it should be a given. Pretty standard nowadays.
They never gave me my $200 deposit back, they said it would take 5-10 days and I tired calling and they never answer!
地點優越，往G-link站僅數分鐘。如果有參加跳傘的話選擇這酒店非常適合，因為會在酒店為接送點。設施較陳舊，但整潔衛生。

We stayed here as part of our Lions Rugby Tour trip and had to be moved after 1 night.  Our hotel room was in a very poor state; the wall lights were loose and the built-in furniture falling to pieces.  We could see the pool area from our balcony and the hot tubs were green and looked like they hadn’t been cleaned and prepared to be overwintered.

As our introduction to 4 star Australian hotels this was not a good one.
Regularly visit for work. Helpful staff
If youre looking for Surfers accom. This is one. Noisy neighbours till 4am but it is close to everything. 2 blocks from Cavill, 100m to the beach, light rail around the corner.

Its always a pleasure staying here, This time it was even a step up.
The service standard was set by the friendly and welcoming gentleman at the drive up/ front door.

My son and I travelled to Surfers Paradise for 2 nights and chose the Mantra On View to stay due to the locality and the very easy access and proximity to the light rail/tram line and nearby Cypress Avenue station. We arrived early and the check in process was so quick and seamless and being early we asked the check in staff if our bags could be held which was absolutely no issue whatsoever. The lady at check in apologised that we could not get into our room before 2pm which I know and accept everywhere I go. I was very happily surprised at about 1230pm I received a call from reception saying my room was ready early if I would like to return and finalise check in - great service thank guys! The room we received despite booking a “City View Room” had a fantastic angled view of the Pacific Ocean, the local riverways and the mountains behind. I’m guessing at some stage there was a coffee table there and the carpet got wet and grew mould. Surely a hazard of this kind is unsuitable for any guest? I kept out of the area entirely as I didn’t want to expose my children to what was potentially toxic mould. Not a small amount either, not that that should matter. Possibly a 60cm round patch and another further towards the window. Again, I want to highlight I am shocked that they’d allow anyone in this room. To me it’s just unacceptable. There were very limited pillows and the bedding wasn’t great at all!! All told. It was bad. I booked this hotel through Accor, and never again, it took 30 minutes on the phone which is just unacceptable. The sales person was just hopeless and had to transfer me, only to start again. Also, when I then looked online, many places had the room available cheaper than through Accor itself. From start to finish my experience with the hotel and Accor itself has been just the worst. I will never stay in another Accor hotel. This just shouldn’t happen. The room had a great view, easy parking, there’d be lots of positive things to say if it wasn’t for the huge mould patch in the carpet!! This was a special treat for myself. As a carer and travelling with two special needs children it should be a given I could trust an establishment like this to make booking easy and provide a safe and comfortable room. That is not what happened. Seriously, do better than this for your guests. I am appalled. Thinking about it, I don’t think I’ve ever stayed somewhere worse.

I paid $150 to be in the heart of Surfers, with a room on the 17th floor (I think there are 20 floors altogether) with sweeping views of the ocean and a little balcony with a small table and chairs to walk out onto. The room was spacious with 2 double beds and a large bathroom with marble tiling. There were ample hand wash and shower gels/ shampoo etc in pump containers. The air con was 'vent in the wall' but had individual controls and worked well.
This was very good value. $150 was a weekday price - Friday, Saturday and Sunday nights are obviously their peak times and the prices rise a fair bit.
The staff at reception were absolutely lovely. If you are wanting newly renovated/ immaculately maintained you will probably need to go to another facility (and pay a hundred or so more per night). I was happy to look past the scuff marks on the doors leading into the rooms for the cheap price, central location and the views.
There is a buffet breakfast (not included, at least at my rate) but I didn't partake of it.
There is limited underground parking which is $20 and in all honesty is a nightmare as there are only steps (quite a lot) up to reception. If arriving by car there is temporary parking in the front loading area/ driveway. I would recommend taking anything you want to take up to your room with you to check in and then coming back down to park the car. Trust me, you will be pleased you did!
I would definitely stay again, but not with my car.
Also, there is no pool (only spa baths/ plunge pools on a deck) but this didn't worry me as I was not interested in this.

What a stunning hotel in superb location! Walked in the door to check in and instantly felt like a place to send a day or two. Easy check in process (super glad we were told about the stairs to car park - luggage would have been difficult to carry up!)
Walked into our room, which looked amazing, well presented and the review from the balcony was just what we expected. Beautiful salty air and the glorious sound of crashing waves. And with only a VERY short walk to because and food options, we were set.

Pros & cons. 2025

We were travelling with a big group. We all had a pretty similar experience at this Mantra.

Pros
- The buffet breakfast had a lot of variety and seating was inside or outside, with some ocean view. $28 per person. And the bin chickens are always entertaining.
- Comfortable beds.
- Easy access.
- 6 minute walk to Coles.
- 3 minute walk to a lovely beach.
- Quality products. Urban Skincare Co. shampoo, conditioner, wash & moisturizer.
- Power board with easy access for phone charging by the beds.
- Almost all the rooms have bathtubs.
- There is a single microwave near reception.
- The large conference room has terrific air-conditioning which was perfect for our dance event. Our organisers brought a dance floor with them and lay it on the carpet.

Cons
- The 'Ocean view' south facing rooms are very noisy, overlooking the neighbouring Cali Beach outdoor pool, which is an outdoor club with music pumping all weekend and late into the nights. Bring good quality earplugs. I believe all the rooms have balconies, but it was much too loud even with the door closed, let alone leaving the door open.
- No hotel pool. Their website is accurate, but the Google photos are out of date, or of the neighbouring pool.
- The four spas vary from completely cold to tepid. The spa heater has apparently been broken for several years.
- The room had a frosted window between the bathroom and the bedroom to help brighten the bathroom. However it meant that if we used the bathroom at night the light would light up the bedroom.
- A microwave in every room should be standard. Lining up for the single microwave at reception was annoying.
- The bath doesn't have anywhere for your head. You can sit in it, but it's uncomfortable to lay in.
- Be sure to turn the mini fridge down low (3) or everything will freeze.

The view from our 'Ocean view' balcony. You can see the ocean through the neighbouring buildings.
great hotel. rooms were clean, view was amazing of the beach and overlooking the heart of Surfers.

PSA This apartment block does not have wifi in all rooms. When you book via booking.com for example they say they provide it - however when rooms are allocated you may receive one which has no wifi. This means there is no tv available either.

My partner and I booked this hotel for our anniversary trip. We were so excited but upon checking in, realised they lied about having 24/7 room service and a functioning jacuzzi spa. Their jacuzzis became faulty due to the storm, the website had not updated that the spas were broken and instead rebranded them as “cold plunge pools” in the hotel but not their website or advertisements.

Furthermore, they admitted that their 24/7 room service meant housekeeping not food and beverage services. When we called to ask for two bottles of water, the receptionist on the phone said “you can go across the street”.

The aircon was turned on but the room was extremely stuffy and would not cool down, it was colder outside of the room in 24°c than it was in the room itself. We requested a room change and they said this wasn’t possible. There was also paint chips on the door, stains on the carpet, huge amounts of dust in the vents, a cockroach on the desk in our room and the walls were extremely thin.

I spoke to 2 different managers expressing my issues and was met with “the aircon is working” “we will give you free breakfast to make up for not having 24h room service” “we will give you access to the pool in another building if you want to swim” despite these options being given to us, we paid a lot of money to stay at a hotel that essentially false advertised. We also stated that had we wanted to use the pool at the adjacent building, we would have stayed there, not at the Mantra on View.

We requested a refund to which the manager stated we needed to consult our third party booking and admitted that they are aware they advertise these services but have not amended their website.

We truly had such a high hopes and pure excitement to stay at this hotel, and felt that the services they stated they had aligned perfectly with what we wanted in our hotel stay for our trip.

My partner and I wound up checking out early, 2 days after checking in for the remaining three nights that we were not there and they will not issue us a refund. The assistant manager I spoke to was extremely rude, dismissive and condescending.

I hope that no other guest experiences such a poor stay at this establishment.

We had a wonderful experience staying at Mantra on View in Gold Coast. The location is fantastic—just a short walk to the beach, shops, and restaurants. The room was clean, spacious, and had a lovely view. The staff were friendly and helpful throughout our stay.

Overall, we were very satisfied and would definitely consider staying here again on our next trip to the Gold Coast. Highly recommended for both families and couples!

We had a great time at this place. We will definitely coming back.

PARKING!!! Be very careful..... The night I checked in the manager had told me to go and park in the upper carpark and bring back the receipt in the morning once you have paid for it, so they can give you refund. They told me if I were to give them the receipt they would cover the cost past the $20 mark. So parking would cost $20 a night in that specific car park. I even repeated myself back to them to make sure I was understanding them correctly. So I did exactly that. The next day I paid $100 in parking!!! Brought the receipt to the reception desk and asked about the refund. But you wait, they then tell me that they don't do refunds, and that they never told me to bring the receipt to them. Such a massive lie on there part. I had told them that I'm now out of pocket more then I should and that they are not communicating the right things. They then told me that I have to contact the car park company to get my money back, but that is not there policy because of miscommunication. Now I can't get my money back, because the hotel refuses to reimburse and the car park people aren't able to refund.

This hotel needs to do better and not screw so many people over.
